followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I revise upward from the prior bundle-limited review because the other reviews highlighted the scrape failure and I checked the full arXiv/PRX text. The central derivations and appendices do exist, so the earlier 'not auditable from bundle' objection should not be treated as a paper-level fatal flaw. I retain the main caveats about approximations, scope, parameter tuning, and experimental reach.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Treating the missing scraped article body as a fatal flaw of the paper itself is too strong once the full arXiv/PRX text is available.
Scoring technical soundness and clarity as zero because of the bundle scrape conflates a data-ingestion failure with the manuscript's content.

The objection that a microscopic model must already recover full GR to be valuable is too strong; the paper clearly frames itself as a nonrelativistic proof-of-concept, though broad popular language should be discounted.
The thermodynamic-limit concern is not wholly unsupported by the paper: the full text contains explicit continuum/free-energy calculations, although their rigor still needs human checking.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The supplied bundle itself is missing the article body, so reviews based only on that bundle necessarily had low confidence.
The result is restricted to nonrelativistic Newtonian gravity, not full general relativity.
The small-wave-packet/small-superposition regime and adiabatic-elimination assumptions are real limitations needing expert checking.
The fluctuation-dissipation/friction remark is a genuine loose end, not merely a stylistic issue.
Prior entropic-gravity test proposals, especially Schimmoller et al. 2021 and Carroll-Remmen-style conceptual critiques, should be part of the novelty/context audit.

main_claims.claim: A nonlocal qubit mediator model can reproduce Newton's two-body and many-body Newtonian force through free-energy extremization after choosing position-dependent mediator frequencies and identifying microscopic parameters with G.
main_claims.notes: The construction is explicit in the full paper, but it is nonlocal and pairwise: the desired radial dependence is placed into mediator frequencies, so the result is best read as a controlled proof-of-concept rather than a natural derivation of gravity from generic microscopic dynamics.
main_claims.paper_location: Section II.1, especially Eqs. 9-17 and 21-23; Appendix A for oscillator analogue

main_claims.claim: A local lattice model of background qubits can generate an attractive Newtonian potential at distances longer than the lattice spacing via a free-energy expansion, with short-distance softening and a special purely entropic point.
main_claims.notes: The mechanism is more physically interesting than the nonlocal construction, but it depends on continuum limits, a small-frequency/free-energy expansion, chemical-potential assumptions, and lattice-scale corrections.

main_claims.claim: Coupling the mediator to a heat bath and adiabatically eliminating bath and mediator degrees of freedom yields an effective Lindblad equation for the masses, with a coherent Newtonian Hamiltonian plus position-basis noise/decoherence.
main_claims.notes: This is the central quantum-mechanical result. The paper gives a detailed derivation, but it is approximation-heavy and includes acknowledged limitations for small wave packets/small superpositions and omitted kinetic-energy-induced friction.

main_claims.claim: The models make observable predictions distinct from ordinary perturbative quantum gravity: force noise, decoherence of spatial superpositions, and altered entanglement-witness behavior.
main_claims.notes: The qualitative discriminants are clear. The quantitative reach is weaker because the nonlocal model has tunable parameters that can suppress noise, while local-model constraints depend on experimental assumptions and omitted environmental channels.

main_claims.claim: The paper is a nonrelativistic Newtonian construction, not a derivation of full general relativity or a complete quantum-gravity theory.
main_claims.notes: The authors are mostly explicit about this limitation, leaving relativistic and self-thermalizing versions for future work. Popular-summary language is broader than the technical result.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A physically motivated origin for the qubits/oscillators, spatial lattice, heat bath, and their position-dependent coupling to matter.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this, the models are controlled analogues of entropic gravity rather than a microscopic account of spacetime degrees of freedom.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Naturalness and universality of the parameter choices that reproduce G, including compatibility with equivalence-principle and many-body constraints.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Section II.1 Eqs. 17 and 23; Section II.2 Eq. 33
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The force law is obtained by fixing combinations of free parameters. Predictive power depends on whether those choices are constrained by deeper principles rather than fitted to Newtonian gravity.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Uniform error estimates for the thermodynamic limit, continuum approximation, free-energy expansion, Born-Markov approximation, and adiabatic elimination.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The observable noise and decoherence effects are meaningful only if approximation errors are smaller than the proposed experimental signatures.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A consistent treatment of kinetic terms, dissipation, and fluctuation-dissipation friction in the same effective dynamics used for experiments.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If the omitted friction or associated heating is too large, it could already rule out parts of the parameter space or alter the phenomenology.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Relativistic extension or causal completion of the local/nonlocal constructions.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Recovering Newtonian gravity is important but does not by itself address Lorentz invariance, gravitational radiation, equivalence-principle structure, or full Einstein dynamics.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the calculations in Sections II.1-II.2 actually give the stated Newtonian sign, normalization, dimensions, and many-body behavior under the parameter identifications in Eqs. 17, 23, and 33?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The ar5iv text available to me omits many displayed mathematical symbols, and the validity depends on detailed integrals and parameter dimensions.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the Appendix C adiabatic elimination mathematically sound as a completely positive Markovian reduction in the claimed regimes, especially for off-diagonal mass density-matrix elements?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires line-by-line checking of the Lindblad perturbation theory and small-superposition expansion by an expert in open quantum systems.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does including kinetic-energy terms recover the fluctuation-dissipation friction with acceptable magnitude across the parameter space shown in Fig. 5?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper acknowledges this as expected but not fully derived in the displayed calculation; assessing it may require extending the calculation.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the experimental numerical bounds robust once realistic atom-interferometer, optomechanical, blackbody, gas-collision, and systematic noise sources are included?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper's estimates deliberately isolate intrinsic gravitational effects, while real feasibility depends on experiment-specific modeling.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: How close is the present construction to prior 'decoherence-free entropic gravity' and classical-channel gravity models, and does it genuinely resolve the Carroll-Remmen entropy critique?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires comparative reading across the cited literature, not just checking the provided paper text.

novelty_assessment: Novelty is fairly high as a concrete nonrelativistic quantum open-system realization of entropic gravity with explicit noise/decoherence phenomenology. It is not first to discuss entropic gravity or even experimental tests of entropic-gravity-like ideas, and the Schimmoller et al. overlap flagged by another reviewer is a real comparison point. Still, the explicit microscopic qubit/oscillator models, Lindblad reduction, and connection to tabletop quantum-gravity tests appear to be a meaningful advance over purely thermodynamic Jacobson/Verlinde-style arguments.
score_rationale: The paper is stronger than my cycle-1 bundle-limited assessment because the full text contains real constructions, derivations, appendices, and quantitative phenomenology. I still do not rate it as technically settled: the main result is nonrelativistic, partly tuned by construction, dependent on open-system approximations, and experimentally distinguishable only in parts of parameter space. Overall it is a promising, publishable proof-of-concept rather than a certified foundation for entropic gravity.
summary: Follow-up assessment after reading the other reviews and checking the full arXiv/PRX text at https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.17575, since the supplied bundle itself omits most equations. The paper is not just an abstract-level claim: it gives explicit nonrelativistic open-quantum-system models whose thermalized qubit or oscillator mediators generate a Newtonian interaction plus noise terms. I do not certify the full derivation. My revised view is that the paper is a serious and novel proof-of-concept, but its strongest claims remain limited by nonrelativistic scope, tuned mediator couplings, adiabatic/Born-Markov approximations, parameter degeneracies, and experimental idealizations.

technical_objections.evidence: The model directly couples qubits to the relative coordinate of a pair of masses, then generalizes to many bodies by repeating the construction for every pair. This limits the explanatory gain over fitting an effective potential, even though it is mathematically a legitimate proof-of-concept.
technical_objections.objection: The nonlocal construction substantially builds in the desired pairwise force through position-dependent mediator frequencies and a separate mediator system for each mass pair.

technical_objections.evidence: The derivation expands the mediator free energy, converts lattice sums to integrals, and recovers Newtonian behavior only beyond the lattice scale, with short-distance softening constrained by fifth-force tests. A human should check the range where all approximations are simultaneously valid.
technical_objections.objection: The local model's Newtonian limit rests on continuum, expansion, and lattice-spacing assumptions whose error control is not fully transparent from the main presentation.

technical_objections.evidence: The authors assume separated bath, mediator, and mass timescales. In the nonlocal appendix they note the absence of an exact steady state for off-diagonal elements, match the Newtonian Hamiltonian to limited order in small superposition size, and state that higher-order extension would be interesting.
technical_objections.objection: The reduced Lindblad dynamics depends critically on Born-Markov and adiabatic-elimination assumptions, and the nonlocal derivation is explicitly limited to small wave packets or small superpositions.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sections II.3-II.4; Appendix C, especially the discussion around Eqs. 40-45 and 127-150

technical_objections.evidence: The paper says the adiabatic elimination should recover friction once kinetic terms are included, and notes that such friction can set independent bounds from stable planetary orbits. This is a real technical loose end for thermodynamic consistency and parameter viability.
technical_objections.objection: Fluctuation-dissipation friction is acknowledged but not derived in the displayed reduced dynamics because kinetic-energy terms are not included in that calculation.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section III.1 footnote on Brownian motion/friction

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states that some parameter regimes become indistinguishable from standard virtual-graviton exchange in the nonrelativistic limit and that no fundamental upper bound on the relevant free parameter has been identified.
technical_objections.objection: The claim of near-term distinguishability is weakened by parameter degeneracies, especially in the nonlocal model where noise/decoherence can be reduced while preserving the Newtonian force.

technical_objections.evidence: The authors explicitly ignore realistic first-decohering errors in the entanglement discussion. That is acceptable for comparing intrinsic gravitational models, but it tempers claims about practical near-term experimental separation.
technical_objections.objection: The experimental entanglement/noise estimates isolate gravitational effects and bracket away important ambient decoherence channels.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section III.3 footnote on ambient gas and blackbody photons; Fig. 5-6 discussion
